query: IMF PortWatch Strait of Hormuz transit calls June 2026 data
answer:
# IMF PortWatch Strait of Hormuz Transit Calls — June 2026

## Summary

The IMF PortWatch platform tracks daily commercial vessel transit calls through the Strait of Hormuz. As of mid-June 2026, **transit calls have been at or near zero** due to the effective closure of the Strait that began on February 28, 2026, following the outbreak of the US-Iran/Israel military conflict. This represents a complete collapse from the pre-war baseline of ~94 vessels per day (7-day moving average).

---

## Key Data Points

### Current PortWatch Reading (Latest Available)
- **Daily transit calls: 0** (as of June 14, 2026 — the most recent PortWatch data point available as of June 17, with ~3-day publication lag)
- **7-day moving average: ~0** (as of June 14, 2026)
- The Strait has been effectively closed for **~112 days** (Feb 28 – Jun 19, 2026)

### Pre-War Baseline (Before Feb 28, 2026)
- **Normal daily transit calls (7-day MA): ~94 vessels/day**
- **Normal oil throughput: ~20.5 million barrels/day**
- **Share of world seaborne oil: ~25%**
- **Share of global LNG: ~20%**

### Data Quality Caveat
PortWatch relies on AIS (Automatic Identification System) transponder data. An estimated **5–10 vessels per day** were transiting under US military escort in mid-June 2026 with AIS disabled or jammed. These do **not** appear in PortWatch counts. The straits.live tracker showed 64 vessels "in transit" and 13 tankers "running dark" — none registering in PortWatch's commercial count.

---

## Context: Why Transit Calls Are Zero

The Strait of Hormuz has been under effective blockade since February 28, 2026:
- **Feb 28**: US-Israel launched "Operation Midnight Hammer"; Iran closed the Strait
- **Apr 13**: US imposed naval blockade of Iranian ports; 50+ vessels redirected
- **May 4**: Trump launched "Project Freedom" naval escort (3 Carrier Strike Groups)
- **Jun 10–11**: IRGC formally declared "complete closure," extending boundary maps into UAE waters
- **Jun 14**: US-Iran MOU digitally signed (Islamabad); PortWatch still recorded 0 transits
- **Jun 17**: Argus Media confirmed "Hormuz tanker traffic unchanged after US-Iran deal"

### Scale of Disruption
- ~2,000 ships stranded (as of Jun 16)
- ~20,000 mariners stranded from 87 countries
- 500+ ships waiting in the Gulf (potential backlog)
- ~2 million bpd global oil supply loss (IEA estimate)
- Described by S&P Global as **"the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy"**

---

## Prediction Markets Using PortWatch as Resolution Source

| Market | Question | Probability (Jun 17) | Volume |
|--------|----------|---------------------|--------|
| Polymarket | 7-day MA ≥ 60 by June 30 | **18.5%** | $24.7M |
| Polymarket | 7-day MA ≥ 60 by July 15 | **38.5%** | $748K |
| Metaculus #44095 | 7-day MA ≥ 30 before June 28 | **~15%** | N/A |

The Polymarket probability for the June 30 threshold spiked to 28.5% on the deal announcement day (Jun 14) then retreated to 18.5% as the deal failed to produce physical traffic.

---

## Reopening Prospects

The **Islamabad MOU** formal signing ceremony was scheduled for **June 19, 2026** (today) at Buergenstock Resort, Switzerland. Major obstacles to traffic recovery:
1. **IRGC non-endorsement** — operationally controls the Strait, has not endorsed the MOU
2. **Mine clearing required** — minesweepers deployed but not yet operational
3. **Insurance frozen** — no commercial coverage available for Hormuz transit
4. **Fitch assessment** — broad reopening unlikely before July 2026
5. **Ongoing attacks** — IRGC nightly drone attacks on ships continued after June 14 deal

---

## PortWatch Access Points
- Main portal: `https://portwatch.imf.org`
- Strait of Hormuz page: `https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730`
- MacroMicro mirror (7-day MA): `https://en.macromicro.me/series/30932/imf-hormuz-number-total-ma7`
- Live tracker: `https://straits.live/`

---

## Data Gaps
- Direct PortWatch readings for June 15–19 could not be retrieved (web fetch tools unavailable)
- Post-signing ceremony (June 19) traffic data not yet available
- PortWatch API documentation not publicly available; data served through portal UI and downloadable CSVs
